title: A Study in Scarlet
author: Doyle, Arthur Conan
summary: A wounded army surgeon, Dr. John H. Watson, returns from the Afghan war to London convalescent and impoverished. Seeking lodgings, he reunites with an acquaintance, Stamford, who introduces him to the brilliant but eccentric chemist Sherlock Holmes. Holmes demonstrates his forensic methods — notably a reagent test for blood — and invites Watson to share rooms in Baker Street. Watson, intrigued, narrates Holmes’s singular habits, experiments with poisons and chemicals, and his passion for exact knowledge, setting the scene for their partnership and forthcoming investigations.

unusual_words.context: Had he discovered a gold mine, greater delight could not have shone upon his features.
